content: If we apply the comparative insight that ICM underperforms Random Network Distillation (RND) and other modern curious agent frameworks in sparse reward open-ended environments to the 2025 use case of configurable ICM for swarm UAV target exploration, we can hypothesize that swapping configurable ICM for a hybrid RND-curious agent intrinsic reward framework will drastically improve swarm UAV target discovery rates in uncharted, low-signal environments. This framework swap leverages the 2025 trend of domain-specific configurable ICM design while addressing a key gap uncovered by head-to-head comparisons of older ICM against newer curiosity mechanisms.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
